---
title: "4.5_spatial_autocorrelation"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-30"
---

```{r}

covariate_standa_df <- read.table("outputs/data_for_models/covariate_standa_df.txt", stringsAsFactors = T)
Y_genus <- read.table("outputs/data_for_models/Y_genus.txt")
```

```{r}
covariate_standa_df %>%
  remove_rownames() %>%
  column_to_rownames("Sample_code") %>%
  dplyr::select(lon, lat)  -> covariate.xy
covariate.xy %>%
  as.matrix() %>%
  dist() -> sample.dist

dist(Y_genus) -> composition.dist
```

```{r}
colnames(sample.dist) == colnames(composition.dist)
rownames(sample.dist) == rownames(composition.dist)
```

```{r}
library(ade4)
library(vegan)

mantel.rtest(sample.dist, composition.dist, nrepet = 9999) -> mantel_test_results
mantel_test_results
plot(mantel_test_results)
```


```{r}
community_distance_hellinger <- vegan::vegdist(Y_genus, method = "hellinger")
community_distance_hellinger
composition.dist
mantel.rtest(sample.dist, community_distance_hellinger, nrepet = 9999) -> mantel_test_helling_results
plot(mantel_test_helling_results)
```

```{r}
genus.hel <- decostand(Y_genus, "hellinger")

genus.hel.resid <- resid(lm(as.matrix(genus.hel) ~ ., data=covariate.xy))


genus.hel.D <- dist(genus.hel.resid)

genus.correlog <- mantel.correlog(community_distance_hellinger, XY=covariate.xy, nperm=999)
summary(genus.correlog)
genus.correlog   
plot(genus.correlog)

genus.correlog2 <- mantel.correlog(community_distance_hellinger, XY=covariate.xy, cutoff=FALSE, 
   r.type="spearman", nperm=999)
summary(genus.correlog2)
genus.correlog2
plot(genus.correlog2)
```
```{r}
load(file = "outputs/models/list_mods_crossvalidation_genus_pois_log.RData")

# 1. Compute predicted values from the fitted model object 'm'
preds <- computePredictedValues(list_mods_crossvalidation[[6]]$m_converg)
str(preds)
# 2. Calculate the posterior mean of the predictions
preds.mean <- apply(preds, FUN = mean, MARGIN =  c(1, 2))
str(preds.mean)
# 3. Calculate raw residuals by subtracting predictions from observed data 'Y'
#    Note: Y must be a matrix matching the model's response structure
raw_residuals <- Y_genus - preds.mean

str(raw_residuals)

genus.res.D <- dist(raw_residuals)

genus_res.correlog <- mantel.correlog(genus.res.D, XY=covariate.xy, nperm=9999)
summary(genus_res.correlog)
genus_res.correlog   
plot(genus_res.correlog)

genus_res.correlog2 <- mantel.correlog(genus.res.D, XY=covariate.xy, cutoff=FALSE, 
   r.type="spearman", nperm=9999)
summary(genus_res.correlog2)
genus_res.correlog2
plot(genus_res.correlog2)
```

```{r}
# 1. Compute predicted values from the fitted model object 'm'
Y <- list_mods[[3]]$m_converg$Y
preds <- computePredictedValues(list_mods[[3]]$m_converg) 
covariate.xy <- list_mods[[3]]$m_converg$XData[,c("lon", "lat")]
str(preds)
# 2. Calculate the posterior mean of the predictions
preds.mean <- apply(preds, FUN = mean, MARGIN =  c(1, 2))
str(preds.mean)
# 3. Calculate raw residuals by subtracting predictions from observed data 'Y'
#    Note: Y must be a matrix matching the model's response structure
raw_residuals <- Y_genus - preds.mean

str(raw_residuals)

genus.res.D <- dist(raw_residuals)

genus_res.correlog <- mantel.correlog(genus.res.D, XY=covariate.xy,cutoff=FALSE, nperm=9999)
summary(genus_res.correlog)
genus_res.correlog   
plot(genus_res.correlog)

genus_res.correlog2 <- mantel.correlog(genus.res.D, XY=covariate.xy, cutoff=FALSE, 
   r.type="spearman", nperm=9999)
summary(genus_res.correlog2)
genus_res.correlog2
plot(genus_res.correlog2)
```
# Convegence check

```{r}
model_name <- list(
  "mod_pois_null",
  "mod_pois_ndvi",
  "mod_pois_precip",
  "mod_pois_tmax",
  "mod_pois_wind",
  "mod_pois_three_precip",
  "mod_pois_three_ndvi",
  "mod_pois_four",
  "mod_pois_full"
)
m = 9
for (m in 3:9){
  convertToCodaObject(list_mods[[m]][[2]]) -> mpost
  gelman.diag(mpost$Beta, multivariate = FALSE)$psrf -> psrf.beta
  effectiveSize(mpost$Beta) -> effective_size_beta
  effective_size_beta %>% hist(breaks = 50, main =model_name[m])
  psrf.beta[,1] %>% hist(breaks = 50, main =model_name[m])
  }
```


# DHARMa

```{r}
library(DHARMa)
?DHARMa
```

```{r}
# =============================================================================
# DHARMa whole-model residual diagnostics for HMSC — fixed spatial test
# Groups by unique lon/lat pairs to guarantee no duplicate coordinates
# =============================================================================

for(m in 1:9){
Y   <- list_mods[[m]]$m_converg$Y
xy  <- list_mods[[m]]$m_converg$XData[, c("lon", "lat")]

preds <- computePredictedValues(list_mods[[m]]$m_converg)

n_sites   <- dim(preds)[1]
n_species <- dim(preds)[2]
n_draws   <- dim(preds)[3]

site_ids  <- rownames(Y)

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Pool all species
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
all_sim_list <- vector("list", n_species)
all_obs_list <- vector("list", n_species)
all_fit_list <- vector("list", n_species)
all_xy_list  <- vector("list", n_species)

for (j in seq_len(n_species)) {

  sim_j <- preds[, j, ]
  obs_j <- Y[, j]
  fit_j <- rowMeans(sim_j)
  keep  <- !is.na(obs_j)

  all_sim_list[[j]] <- sim_j[keep, , drop = FALSE]
  all_obs_list[[j]] <- obs_j[keep]
  all_fit_list[[j]] <- fit_j[keep]
  all_xy_list[[j]]  <- xy[keep, , drop = FALSE]
}
length(all_sim_list)
all_sim_matrix <- do.call(rbind, all_sim_list)
all_obs_vector <- do.call(c,     all_obs_list)
all_fit_vector <- do.call(c,     all_fit_list)
all_xy_df      <- do.call(rbind, all_xy_list)   # n_valid_obs × 2, coords repeated per species

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Create DHARMa object
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
str(all_sim_matrix)
dharma_global <- createDHARMa(
  simulatedResponse       = all_sim_matrix,
  observedResponse        = all_obs_vector,
  fittedPredictedResponse = all_fit_vector,
  integerResponse         = FALSE
)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Standard DHARMa diagnostics
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
plot(dharma_global, main = "DHARMa residuals — whole HMSC model")
 
testUniformity(dharma_global)    # KS test: should be uniform [0,1]
testDispersion(dharma_global)    # over/under-dispersion
testOutliers(dharma_global)      # outlier fraction
 
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Group by unique lon/lat pairs
#
# Some site IDs may share identical coordinates → grouping by site ID still
# leaves duplicate x,y values for testSpatialAutocorrelation.
# Solution: build the group key directly from coordinate strings.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
coord_key    <- paste(all_xy_df[, "lon"], all_xy_df[, "lat"], sep = "_")
coord_factor <- factor(coord_key)

cat("Unique coordinate groups:", nlevels(coord_factor), "\n")
cat("Total pooled observations:", length(all_obs_vector), "\n\n")

# Aggregate residuals to one per unique location
dharma_per_site <- recalculateResiduals(
  dharma_global,
  group = coord_factor
)

# Extract one coordinate row per level, in the same order as factor levels
level_idx <- match(levels(coord_factor), coord_key)
xy_unique  <- all_xy_df[level_idx, ]

cat("Rows in xy_unique:", nrow(xy_unique), "\n")
cat("Any duplicate coordinates remaining:", anyDuplicated(xy_unique) > 0, "\n\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Spatial autocorrelation test — now guaranteed unique coordinates
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
testSpatialAutocorrelation(
  dharma_per_site,
  x = xy_unique[, "lon"],
  y = xy_unique[, "lat"]
)}
```


```{r}
Y <- list_mods[[6]]$m_converg$Y
xy <- list_mods[[6]]$m_converg$XData[,c("lon", "lat")]
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Compute posterior predictive draws
#    preds has dimensions [sites x species x posterior_draws]
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
preds <- computePredictedValues(list_mods[[6]]$m_converg) 
 
cat("preds dimensions (sites x species x posterior_draws):\n")
cat(dim(preds), "\n\n")
 
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Run DHARMa diagnostics — one species at a time
#
#    createDHARMa() requires:
#      simulatedResponse : matrix [n_obs  x  n_simulations]  ← preds[, j, ]
#      observedResponse  : vector [n_obs]                    ← Y[, j]
#      fittedPredictedResponse : vector [n_obs] (posterior mean, optional but recommended)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
 
dharma_results <- vector("list", ncol(Y))
names(dharma_results) <- colnames(Y)
 
for (j in seq_len(ncol(Y))) {
 
  sp_name <- colnames(Y)[j]
  cat("Processing species:", sp_name, "\n")
 
  # Posterior draws for species j → [n_sites x n_draws] matrix
  sim_matrix <- preds[, j, ]          # dim: [50 x 400]
 
  # Posterior mean (used as fittedPredictedResponse)
  fitted_mean <- rowMeans(sim_matrix) # length 50
 
  # Observed values for species j → vector
  obs_vector  <- Y[, j]              # length 50
  
  sim_matrix <- sim_matrix[!is.na(obs_vector),]
  fitted_mean <- fitted_mean[!is.na(obs_vector)]
  xy_temp <- xy[!is.na(obs_vector),]
  obs_vector <- obs_vector[!is.na(obs_vector)]

  # obs_vector[is.na(obs_vector)] <- 0
  dharma_results[[j]] <- createDHARMa(
    simulatedResponse      = sim_matrix,
    observedResponse       = obs_vector,
    fittedPredictedResponse = fitted_mean,
    integerResponse        = F      # TRUE for counts/presence-absence
  )
  print(testSpatialAutocorrelation(dharma_results[[j]], x = xy_temp[,"lon"], y = xy_temp[,"lat"]))
}

str(obs_vector)
```


```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Inspect results for a single species
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
sp_to_plot <- 1   # change index to inspect other species
 
cat("\n--- DHARMa diagnostics for:", names(dharma_results)[sp_to_plot], "---\n")
 
# Standard DHARMa plot (uniformity + dispersion + outliers)
plot(dharma_results[[sp_to_plot]],
     main = paste("DHARMa residuals —", names(dharma_results)[sp_to_plot]))
 
# Individual tests
testUniformity(dharma_results[[sp_to_plot]])   # KS test for uniform distribution
testDispersion(dharma_results[[sp_to_plot]])   # over/under-dispersion
testOutliers(dharma_results[[sp_to_plot]])     # outlier detection
```

```{r}
for(sp in 1:length(dharma_results)){
plot(dharma_results[[sp]],
     main = paste("DHARMa residuals —", names(dharma_results)[sp]))
  testDispersion(dharma_results[[sp]])

  }
``` 
```{r include=FALSE, purl = FALSE}
list_mods[[3]]$m_converg -> m_converg_selected # pois_full_log_NA
postBeta2 <- getPostEstimate(m_converg_selected, parName = "Beta")
beta_results <- postBeta2$mean
mpost2 <- convertToCodaObject(m_converg_selected)
gelman.diag(mpost2$Beta, multivariate = FALSE) -> gelman_diag_df2
effectiveSize(mpost2$Beta) -> effective_size_beta
effective_size_beta %>%hist(, breaks = 50)
gelman_diag_df2$psrf[,1] %>% hist(, breaks = 50)
Hmsc::computeWAIC(list_mods[[3]]$m_converg)
gelman.diag(mpost2$Rho, multivariate = FALSE)
effectiveSize(mpost2$Rho)
```


# Binary model

```{r}
load(file = "outputs/models/list_mods_bin_autocorr_genus.RData")
```

```{r}
Y_bin <- list_mods_bin[[6]]$m_converg$Y
xy <- list_mods_bin[[6]]$m_converg$XData[,c("lon", "lat")]
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Compute posterior predictive draws
#    preds has dimensions [sites x species x posterior_draws]
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
preds_bin <- computePredictedValues(list_mods_bin[[6]]$m_converg) 

cat("preds dimensions (sites x species x posterior_draws):\n")
cat(dim(preds), "\n\n")
 
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Run DHARMa diagnostics — one species at a time
#
#    createDHARMa() requires:
#      simulatedResponse : matrix [n_obs  x  n_simulations]  ← preds[, j, ]
#      observedResponse  : vector [n_obs]                    ← Y[, j]
#      fittedPredictedResponse : vector [n_obs] (posterior mean, optional but recommended)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
 
dharma_results_bin <- vector("list", ncol(Y_bin))
names(dharma_results_bin) <- colnames(Y_bin)
 
for (j in seq_len(ncol(Y_bin))) {
 
  sp_name <- colnames(Y_bin)[j]
  cat("Processing species:", sp_name, "\n")
 
  # Posterior draws for species j → [n_sites x n_draws] matrix
  sim_matrix_bin <- +(preds_bin[, j, ]>0.7 )         # dim: [50 x 400]
 
  # Posterior mean (used as fittedPredictedResponse)
  fitted_med_bin <-apply(sim_matrix_bin, 1, median) 
 
  # Observed values for species j → vector
  obs_vector_bin  <- Y_bin[, j]              # length 50
  
  # sim_matrix_bin <- sim_matrix_bin[!is.na(obs_vector_bin),]
  # fitted_mean_bin <- fitted_mean_bin[!is.na(obs_vector_bin)]
  # xy_temp <- xy[!is.na(obs_vector_bin),]
  # obs_vector_bin <- obs_vector[!is.na(obs_vector_bin)]

  # obs_vector[is.na(obs_vector)] <- 0
  dharma_results_bin[[j]] <- createDHARMa(
    simulatedResponse      = sim_matrix_bin,
    observedResponse       = obs_vector_bin,
    fittedPredictedResponse = fitted_med_bin,
    integerResponse        = T      # TRUE for counts/presence-absence
  )
  #print(testSpatialAutocorrelation(dharma_results_bin[[j]], x = xy[,"lon"], y = xy[,"lat"]))
}

```


```{r}
for(sp in 1:length(dharma_results_bin)){
plot(dharma_results_bin[[sp]],
     main = paste("DHARMa residuals —", names(dharma_results_bin)[sp]))
  }
```
